question_text: A radio station broadcasts at a frequency of 100 MHz. Using the equation c = fλ, where c = 3 × 10⁸ m/s, calculate the wavelength of the radio waves in metres.
explanation: Rearrange c = fλ to get λ = c/f. Convert 100 MHz to 100 × 10⁶ Hz = 1 × 10⁸ Hz. Then λ = (3 × 10⁸) / (1 × 10⁸) = 3 m.
